title: rosa communis var. subbiserrata
intro: Rosa communis var. subbiserrata, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a flowering plant species within the Rosa genus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rosa communis var. subbiserrata include reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, and it is also used in the production of rose hips, a rich source of vitamins and minerals.
